Authored by GPT-5 (Codex Desktop). Session 22713fa8-23d2-4b31-918b-6e2f48d69c06.

Resolves #11190

Adds the archive-tier-only `archivePath()` helper for Epic #11187 Phase 1 AC2. The helper formats paths under `archive/{type}/{version-or-bucket}/`, keeps buckets flat through the configured 100-item threshold, switches to sealed ordinal `chunk-N/` folders above the threshold, and leaves active-tier `chunkPath(id)` ID-range semantics untouched.

Evidence: L1 (unit-tested pure path helper) -> L1 required (path contract ACs only). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket (if any)

None. The implementation follows the ticket's explicit caller-owned bucket-state shape and avoids filesystem scans inside the formatter.

## Test Evidence

- `npm run test-unit -- test/playwright/unit/ai/services/github-workflow/ArchivePath.spec.mjs` -> 6 passed
- `git diff --cached --check` -> passed before commit
- `git diff --check origin/dev...HEAD` -> passed before push

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] AC3-AC5 service refactors consume `archivePath()` for archive-write paths while preserving active `chunkPath(id)` lookup semantics.

## Related

- Parent Epic: #11187
- AC1 Config lane: #11189 / PR #11191
- Discussion: #11180

## Commit

- `21219bd8e` — `feat(github-workflow): add archive path helper (#11190)`

> ### 🔬 Depth Floor
> 
> **Documented search (per guide §7.1):**
> 
> I actively looked for:
> 1. **Active-tier contamination** — confirmed `archivePath.mjs` imports only `path` (no `chunkPath` dependency); test line 126 explicitly verifies `chunkPath(11190)` returns `'111xx'` unchanged after `archivePath` invocation. ✓
> 2. **Hidden filesystem scans inside the helper** — confirmed pure-function shape; `itemCount` + `itemIndex` caller-owned per #11190 AC explicitly. No `fs` import. ✓
> 3. **Path-traversal vulnerabilities** — `validateSegment` rejects `/`, `\`, `..` exact, `..` substring; `allowPath: true` only on `archiveRoot` (the one segment that legitimately spans subdirectories). Verified the `..` substring check covers e.g. `'issues..hidden'` not just `'..'`. ✓
> 4. **Sealed-chunk semantic boundary** — `Math.floor(itemIndex / 100) + 1` correctly seals chunk-1 at index 99 + opens chunk-2 at index 100. Verified mathematically; test covers itemCount=101 / itemIndex=100 → chunk-2. ✓
> 5. **Non-release bucket support for rejected PRs** (Discussion #11180 OQ1 RESOLVED) — `bucket: 'rejected'` path covered + tested at line 72-80. ✓
> 6. **Mutual-exclusivity of `version` and `bucket`** — `validateBucket` enforces exactly-one via `(version && bucket) || (!version && !bucket)`. Tested at line 90-98 with both-provided case throwing `/exactly one/`. ✓
> 
> One **follow-up observation** (non-blocking, would surface as Cycle N+1 elaboration if pursued):
> 
> - **`maxItemsPerDir` override not tested with non-default value.** The constant is exported + the parameter is exposed, but the 6 boundary tests all use the default 100. If a future caller passes `maxItemsPerDir: 50` (e.g., for finer-grained portal-UI testing), the math still holds, but no test pins it. Adding `maxItemsPerDir: 5, itemCount: 6, itemIndex: 5 → chunk-2` would be belt+suspenders. Non-blocking; could land in AC3-AC5 follow-ups if a real caller surfaces.
>

> 
> [Closing Remarks]
> 
> Strong AC2 foundation. The mathematics-of-chunking is correctly extracted as a pure function — the "where does an item go" decision is fully decided by `(version-or-bucket, itemCount, itemIndex, maxItemsPerDir)` with zero filesystem dependency. That's the right shape for AC3-AC5 to consume: each service computes its planned bucket state once, then makes N path-format calls without each one re-scanning disk. As AC3 lane-owner, this is exactly the contract I need.
> 
> Will run `gh pr review --approve` referencing this comment URL post-A2A handoff per §2.7.
